docs: The [Self::obligation]'s owner.
accounts.name: owner

docs: The obligation to set the [BorrowOrder] on.
accounts.name: obligation

docs: The [Self::obligation]'s market - needed to validate feature flags and minimum order value.
accounts.name: lendingMarket

docs: The reserve matching the [Self::debt_liquidity_mint] - needed for the minimum order value
check. On cancellation, this account is ignored (but still must belong to the same market).
accounts.name: reserve

docs: The [BorrowOrder::filled_debt_destination] to set on order creation. Not editable on order
updates.
Ignored when cancelling the order.
accounts.name: filledDebtDestination

docs: The [BorrowOrder::debt_liquidity_mint] to set on order creation. Not editable on order
updates.
Ignored when cancelling the order.
accounts.name: debtLiquidityMint

docs: Obligation's owner (signer - only the owner can modify the obligation's configuration).
accounts.name: owner

docs: The obligation to update.
accounts.name: obligation

docs: The reserve of a selected one among the [Obligation::borrows].
Required only if the config update affects a single borrow (for validating its new config).
accounts.name: borrowReserve

docs: The reserve of a selected one among the [Obligation::deposits].
Required only if the config update affects a single deposit (for validating its new config).
accounts.name: depositReserve

docs: The lending market (for validating the obligation's new config).
accounts.name: lendingMarket

docs: The obligation with a borrow to be rolled over (or migrated).
accounts.name: obligation

docs: The [Self::obligation]'s market, needed for validations against the market-wide
configuration.
accounts.name: lendingMarket

docs: The [Self::lending_market]'s authority, needed to transfer the newly-borrowed funds out of
the [Self::reserve_source_liquidity].
accounts.name: lendingMarketAuthority

docs: The reserve used by the existing [Self::obligation]'s borrow that needs to rollover from.
accounts.name: sourceBorrowReserve

docs: The reserve to rollover the borrow into - possibly (but not necessarily) the same as
[Self::source_borrow_reserve]'s (but definitely of the same liquidity mint).
accounts.name: targetBorrowReserve

docs: The mint of [Self::source_borrow_reserve] and [Self::target_borrow_reserve], needed to
transfer funds between them.
accounts.name: liquidityMint

docs: The vault of [Self::source_borrow_reserve], from which the funds are transferred.
accounts.name: sourceBorrowReserveLiquidity

docs: The vault of [Self::target_borrow_reserve], from which the funds are transferred.
accounts.name: targetBorrowReserveLiquidity

docs: The token program of [Self::liquidity_mint] - needed to execute the transfer.
accounts.name: tokenProgram

docs: The obligation with a [BorrowOrder].
accounts.name: obligation

docs: The [Self::obligation]'s market - needed for borrowing-related configuration.
accounts.name: lendingMarket

docs: The [Self::lending_market]'s authority, needed to transfer the newly-borrowed funds out of
the [Self::reserve_source_liquidity].
accounts.name: lendingMarketAuthority

docs: The reserve to borrow from.
Its mint must match the asset requested by the [BorrowOrder::debt_liquidity_mint].
accounts.name: borrowReserve

docs: The mint of [Self::borrow_reserve] - needed to execute the transfer.
accounts.name: borrowReserveLiquidityMint

docs: The vault of [Self::borrow_reserve], from which the funds are transferred.
accounts.name: reserveSourceLiquidity

docs: The fee vault of [Self::borrow_reserve], to which the fees are transferred.
accounts.name: borrowReserveLiquidityFeeReceiver

docs: The destination token account that should receive the newly borrowed funds.
It must match [BorrowOrder::filled_debt_destination], owner and mint.
**Warning:** An altered destination account will prevent an order from being filled.
accounts.name: userDestinationLiquidity

docs: The referrer's account, for accumulating fees - needed if the [Obligation::has_referrer].
accounts.name: referrerTokenState

docs: The token program of [Self::borrow_reserve] - needed to execute the transfer.
accounts.name: tokenProgram

docs: The depositor holding ctokens.
accounts.name: owner

docs: The market's authority, needed to initialize the [Self::owner_queued_collateral_vault].
accounts.name: lendingMarketAuthority

docs: The source of collateral to be enqueued.
accounts.name: userSourceCollateralTa

docs: The account to which the liquidity should be finally transferred later (to be recorded in
the ticket).
accounts.name: userDestinationLiquidityTa

docs: The collateral's program - needed for invoking the transfer to the vault *and* (implicitly)
for handling the `init_if_needed`.
accounts.name: collateralTokenProgram

docs: The new account to be initialized with the issued ticket's data.
accounts.name: withdrawTicket

docs: The per-owner "this reserve's queued collateral" vault (in which the collateral will be
locked).
accounts.name: ownerQueuedCollateralVault

docs: The System program - needed only for `init` / `init_if_needed` of the accounts above.
accounts.name: systemProgram

docs: The first one out of maximum 2 custom accounts that may be required if the optional "ticket
progress callback" is configured for this ticket.
The expected custom accounts are validated based on the `progress_callback_type` instruction
argument. The currently supported ones are:
- for [ProgressCallbackType::None] no custom accounts are expected (this is the default
behavior, when the instruction data is zeroed or absent).
- for [ProgressCallbackType::KlendQueueAccountingHandlerOnKvault], only the `_0` custom
account is required:
- the `_0` custom account must represent the `VaultState` which disinvests from the
reserve (readonly; needed to be later passed as an input to the Kvault's handler);
- the `_1` custom account is ignored;
- the `owner` account (i.e. the signer) must be a Kvault-owned PDA base authority
associated with the vault indicated by `_0` (needed to prove that Kvault is CPI'ing this
handler).
accounts.name: progressCallbackCustomAccount0

docs: The second possible account (see the `progress_callback_custom_account_0` above).
accounts.name: progressCallbackCustomAccount1

docs: The executor of the permissionless tx (not necessarily the ticket owner).
accounts.name: payer

docs: The lending market.
accounts.name: lendingMarket

docs: The market's authority, needed for burning the collateral (from
[Self::owner_queued_collateral_vault]) and transferring the liquidity (from
[Self::reserve_liquidity_supply]).
accounts.name: lendingMarketAuthority

docs: The reserve.
accounts.name: reserve

docs: The liquidity mint, needed to invoke the transfer.
accounts.name: reserveLiquidityMint

docs: The collateral mint, needed to burn (`mut`!) the queued collateral.
accounts.name: reserveCollateralMint

docs: The liquidity supply vault (to withdraw the liquidity from).
accounts.name: reserveLiquiditySupply

docs: The per-owner "this reserve's queued collateral" vault (from which the collateral will be
burnt).
accounts.name: ownerQueuedCollateralVault

docs: The token account to which the liquidity should be transferred (the one recorded in the
ticket).
accounts.name: userDestinationLiquidity

docs: The program of [Self::reserve_collateral_mint], needed for transfer.
accounts.name: collateralTokenProgram

docs: The program of [Self::reserve_liquidity_mint], needed for transfer.
accounts.name: liquidityTokenProgram

docs: The ticket's data itself.
Note: in case of complete withdrawal, this account will be closed. In case of partial
withdrawal, its [WithdrawTicket::queued_collateral_amount] will simply be reduced, and the
ticket will maintain its position in the queue.
accounts.name: withdrawTicket

docs: The owner of the [Self::withdraw_ticket]; needed only to return the rent of the
[WithdrawTicket] account (if it is getting fully-consumed and closed here).
accounts.name: withdrawTicketOwner

docs: The ATA program - needed for potential destination ATA creation.
accounts.name: associatedTokenProgram

docs: The System program - needed for potential destination ATA creation.
accounts.name: systemProgram

docs: The progress callback program (if configured by the withdraw ticket).
accounts.name: progressCallbackProgram

docs: The first one out of maximum 2 custom accounts that may be required if the withdraw ticket
defines a callback.
Please note that the constraints defined here do not mention `mut`, but a specific
[WithdrawTicket::progress_callback_type] may require it.
accounts.name: progressCallbackCustomAccount0

docs: The second possibly-required account (see the `progress_callback_custom_account_0` above).
accounts.name: progressCallbackCustomAccount1

docs: The transaction executor.
This instruction is, in principle, permissionless. However, only the ticket owner can use
arbitrary token account as destination for recovered collateral. Other signers can only
transfer the collateral to the ticket owner's ATA (see [Self::user_source_collateral]).
accounts.name: payer

docs: The lending market.
accounts.name: lendingMarket

docs: The market's authority, needed for transferring the collateral (from
[Self::owner_queued_collateral_vault]).
accounts.name: lendingMarketAuthority

docs: The reserve, needed only to validate the other accounts.
accounts.name: reserve

docs: The collateral mint, needed to invoke the transfer.
accounts.name: reserveCollateralMint

docs: The per-owner "this reserve's queued collateral" vault (from which the collateral will be
recovered).
accounts.name: ownerQueuedCollateralVault

docs: The ticket's owner token account to which the ticket-locked collateral should be returned.
Only the ticket's owner can indicate an arbitrary token account here. Permissionless
executors must indicate the ticket's owner ATA.
accounts.name: userSourceCollateral

docs: The program of [Self::reserve_collateral_mint], needed for transfer.
accounts.name: collateralTokenProgram

docs: The ticket's account, necessarily marked as [WithdrawTicket::invalid] first (by the
`handler_withdraw_queued_liquidity`).
accounts.name: withdrawTicket

docs: The owner of the [Self::withdraw_ticket]; needed only to return the rent of the
[WithdrawTicket] account.
accounts.name: withdrawTicketOwner
